title: wonton chinese puff pastry chow-fun
description: a light and comforting chinese dish featuring puff pastry and a tangy yuzu-cure, served in an agave leaf
instructions: roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
cut out 12-15 rectangles from the puff pastry, about 3 inches (7.5 cm) wide and 4 inches (10 cm) long.
place a wonton wrapper in the center of each rectangle, leaving a 1/2 inch (1 cm) border around the wonton wrapper.
brush the edges of the puff pastry with a little water, then fold the pastry over the wonton wrapper to form a triangle or a square, and press the edges to seal.
brush the tops of the pastry with a little water, then sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
place the pastries on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each pastry.
bake the pastries for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
meanwhile, prepare the yuzu-cure by whisking together 1/4 cup yuzu juice, 1/4 cup soy sauce, and 1 tablespoon honey in a small bowl until smooth.
add the crumbled tofu to the yuzu-cure and mix until well combined.
to assemble the dish, place a few of the pastries in the center of each agave leaf, then spoon some of the yuzu-tofu mixture over the pastries.
garnish with chopped scallions and serve immediately
